state_description: Waiting for human approval of the plan
product_description: School ERP system with student enrollment, attendance tracking, grade management, timetable scheduling, parent communication, and fee collection.

plan.description: Gather and document business requirements for all six School ERP modules: student enrollment, attendance tracking, grade management, timetable scheduling, parent communication, and fee collection. Produce user stories, process flow diagrams, and ROI analysis.
acceptance_criteria: User stories written for all six modules with acceptance criteria
Process flow diagrams cover end-to-end workflows for each module
ROI analysis includes cost savings and efficiency gains
Stakeholder sign-off documented

plan.description: Produce the Product Requirements Document (PRD), phased roadmap, prioritization matrix, and success metrics for the School ERP system. Define MVP scope vs. future phases.
acceptance_criteria: PRD covers all six modules with detailed feature requirements
Roadmap distinguishes MVP from Phase 2 with release milestones
Success metrics defined: enrollment time reduction, attendance accuracy ≥99%, parent engagement rate
Prioritization matrix approved by product owner

plan.description: Draft Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and Data Processing Agreements compliant with FERPA (student records), COPPA (children under 13), and applicable state education privacy laws. Define data retention and deletion policies.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy policy explicitly addresses FERPA student record protections
COPPA compliance verified for any features accessible to students under 13
Data retention and deletion schedules documented per FERPA 5-year rule
DPA template ready for school district countersignature
FERPA/COPPA compliance if minors involved

plan.description: Conduct user research with school admins, teachers, parents, and students. Produce personas, journey maps, and an initial information architecture for all six modules.
acceptance_criteria: Minimum 5 user interviews conducted per persona type
Journey maps cover end-to-end flows for all six modules
Information architecture validated with representative users
Accessibility requirements captured for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
Accessibility for learners with disabilities
Mobile-responsive for student devices

plan.description: Produce a threat model and security design for the School ERP system covering student PII protection, role-based access control, data encryption at rest and in transit, and audit logging requirements.
acceptance_criteria: STRIDE threat model covers all six ERP modules
RBAC matrix defines permissions for: super_admin, school_admin, teacher, parent, student
Encryption requirements specified: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
Audit log requirements defined for FERPA compliance
Security design reviewed and approved

plan.description: Create compliance evidence artifacts for FERPA, COPPA, and WCAG 2.1. Produce a traceability matrix mapping each standard's requirements to system features, and a compliance checklist for pre-launch audit.
acceptance_criteria: FERPA traceability matrix maps all 34 CFR Part 99 requirements to system controls
COPPA checklist covers parental consent workflows and data minimization
WCAG 2.1 AA audit plan defines test cases for all UI components
Risk register identifies compliance gaps with remediation owners

plan.description: Design wireframes and high-fidelity mockups for all six modules across three user portals: Admin Portal, Teacher Portal, and Parent/Student Portal. Include accessibility annotations for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
acceptance_criteria: Wireframes cover all screens for all six modules across three portals
Design system defines color palette, typography, spacing, and component library
All interactive elements have accessibility annotations (ARIA labels, focus order, contrast ratios)
Mockups reviewed and approved by representative users from each persona
Color contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 for all text per WCAG 2.1 AA
Accessibility for learners with disabilities
Mobile-responsive for student devices

plan.description: Design the relational database schema for all School ERP modules. Define tables, relationships, indexes, and migration scripts. Schema must support multi-school tenancy, soft deletes for FERPA compliance, and full audit trail.
acceptance_criteria: Schema supports multi-school tenancy via school_id foreign key on all tenant tables
All student/guardian PII tables have soft delete (deleted_at) for FERPA record retention
Audit log table captures: user_id, action, table_name, record_id, old_value, new_value, timestamp
Indexes defined on all foreign keys and high-frequency query columns
Migration scripts are idempotent and reversible
Schema ERD diagram produced and reviewed

plan.description: Design the OpenAPI 3.0 specification for all School ERP REST API endpoints covering authentication, enrollment, attendance, grades, timetable, messaging, and fee collection. Define request/response schemas, error codes, and rate limits.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints documented with request/response schemas and example payloads
Authentication endpoints: login, logout, refresh, password-reset
RBAC enforcement documented per endpoint (which roles can call what)
Pagination, filtering, and sorting parameters defined for list endpoints
Error response schema standardized across all endpoints
OpenAPI spec validates with no errors against swagger-parser

plan.description: Set up the monorepo project structure, development environment configurations, environment variable management, Docker Compose for local development, and toolchain for backend (Python/FastAPI) and frontend (React/TypeScript).

payload.backend_stack: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, Celery, Redis

acceptance_criteria: docker-compose up starts all services with no manual steps
Makefile provides: make dev, make test, make migrate, make lint targets
.env.example documents all required environment variables with descriptions
Backend linting (ruff) and formatting (black) configured
Frontend linting (ESLint) and formatting (Prettier) configured
Pre-commit hooks enforcing lint/format checks

plan.description: Set up i18n/l10n infrastructure for the School ERP system. Configure react-i18next for the frontend and a translation management system. Produce initial English locale strings and translation scaffolds for Spanish and French.
acceptance_criteria: All UI strings externalized into locale JSON files — zero hardcoded strings in components
react-i18next configured with namespace-based loading per module
English locale 100% complete; Spanish and French scaffolds with keys present
RTL layout support scaffolded for future locale expansion
Date, time, currency, and number formatting use locale-aware formatters
Translation key naming convention documented in CONTRIBUTING.md

plan.description: Implement the authentication and authorization service: JWT login/logout/refresh, RBAC enforcement middleware, password reset flow, session management, and multi-school tenant context resolution.
acceptance_criteria: POST /auth/login returns access token (15min) and refresh token (7d)
POST /auth/refresh rotates refresh token and returns new access token
RBAC middleware enforces role permissions on all protected endpoints
Password reset sends email with time-limited token (1hr expiry)
Tenant context resolved from JWT claim and propagated to all DB queries
Failed login attempts rate-limited to 5 per minute per IP

plan.description: Implement the Student Enrollment module: student registration, guardian linkage, document upload, enrollment into academic year/section, and transfer/withdrawal workflows.
acceptance_criteria: POST /students creates student record with validation and returns student_id
Guardian linkage supports multiple guardians per student with relationship type
Document upload accepts PDF/JPG/PNG, stores to S3, returns signed URL
Enrollment assigns student to academic_year + grade_level + section
Transfer workflow creates audit trail and notifies receiving school admin
Withdrawal records reason, date, and preserves historical records per FERPA

plan.description: Implement the Attendance Tracking module: daily and period-based attendance marking, bulk attendance upload, absentee notifications to parents, attendance reports, and analytics.
acceptance_criteria: POST /attendance/mark accepts class_id, date, and student status list (present/absent/late/excused)
Bulk CSV upload processes 500-student class in under 5 seconds
Parent notification sent via email/SMS within 60 seconds of absence marked
GET /attendance/reports returns attendance percentage per student per date range
Consecutive absence alert triggers after configurable threshold (default: 3 days)
Attendance records are immutable after 48-hour edit window (audit requirement)

plan.description: Implement the Grade Management module: assignment/exam creation, grade entry by teachers, weighted GPA calculation, report card generation, and grade history tracking.
acceptance_criteria: Assessments support configurable weight (e.g., homework 20%, midterm 30%, final 50%)
Grade entry validates score against max_score and flags outliers
GPA calculated using weighted average across all subjects for the term
Report card PDF generated with school branding, student photo, and grade breakdown
Grade history is append-only; teacher edits create a new revision with reason
GET /grades/student/{id} returns all grades with term filtering

plan.description: Implement the Timetable Scheduling module: period/bell schedule configuration, subject-teacher-room assignment, conflict detection, timetable publication, and substitution management.
acceptance_criteria: Conflict detection prevents teacher or room double-booking and returns specific conflict details
Timetable generation validates subject frequency constraints per academic year config
Published timetable is versioned; changes create new draft until re-published
Substitution records original teacher, substitute teacher, and reason
GET /timetable/class/{id} returns weekly schedule in structured JSON
Timetable export to iCal format for calendar integration

plan.description: Implement the Parent Communication module: in-app messaging between teachers and parents, school-wide announcements, push/email notifications, message threading, and read receipts.
acceptance_criteria: Direct messages support teacher↔parent and admin↔parent threads
Announcements support targeting: school-wide, grade-level, or section-specific
Push notifications delivered via Firebase Cloud Messaging within 30 seconds
Email notifications use school-branded templates via SendGrid/SES
Read receipts update when message is opened by recipient
Message content moderation flag available for school admins
FERPA: message metadata logged; content accessible to school admin on request

plan.description: Implement the Fee Collection module: fee structure configuration, invoice generation, online payment processing (Stripe), partial payment support, overdue reminders, and financial reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Fee structures support one-time and recurring (monthly/quarterly/annual) fees
Invoices auto-generated at billing period start and sent to guardian email
Stripe webhook handles: payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed
Partial payment records amount_paid, remaining_balance, and payment_date
Overdue reminders sent at: 7, 14, and 30 days after due date
Financial report shows: collected, outstanding, and overdue amounts per class/school
Payment receipts generated as PDF and emailed to guardian

plan.description: Build the React frontend foundation: routing, authentication flow (login/logout/password-reset), role-based layout (Admin Portal, Teacher Portal, Parent Portal), global state management, and API client with JWT handling.

payload.stack: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, TailwindCSS, React Router v6, Zustand, React Query

acceptance_criteria: Login page collects email/password, shows validation errors, redirects by role on success
JWT stored in httpOnly cookie; API client auto-refreshes on 401
ProtectedRoute redirects unauthenticated users to login
RoleGate conditionally renders UI elements based on user role
Role-based navigation shows only permitted menu items per role
React Query configured with stale time, retry policy, and error boundaries
All text rendered via react-i18next — zero hardcoded strings

plan.description: Build the Student Enrollment UI: student registration form, guardian management, document upload, enrollment wizard, student directory with search/filter, and student profile page.
acceptance_criteria: Enrollment wizard is multi-step with progress indicator and back/next navigation
Document uploader shows upload progress, file preview, and error states
Student directory supports search by name/ID and filter by grade/section/status
Student profile displays all data sections with inline edit capability for admins
All form fields have associated labels and error messages for screen readers
Keyboard navigation works for all interactive elements (Tab, Enter, Space, Escape)

plan.description: Build the Attendance Tracking UI: class attendance marking interface with bulk select, attendance calendar view per student, attendance reports dashboard, and absentee notification history.
acceptance_criteria: Attendance grid lists all students in class with one-click present/absent/late/excused toggle
Bulk select marks all as present then allows individual exceptions
Student attendance calendar shows color-coded status for each school day
Reports dashboard shows attendance percentage chart filterable by date range and class
Notification history shows which parents were notified and when
Submit button disabled after 48-hour edit window with tooltip explanation

plan.description: Build the Grade Management UI: teacher grade entry interface, assessment creation, student grade book view, report card preview and download, and grade analytics charts.
acceptance_criteria: Grade book table supports inline grade entry with tab-key navigation between cells
Assessment form validates weight percentages sum to 100% before save
Grade distribution chart shows class performance histogram per assessment
Report card PDF preview renders in-browser before download
Student grade view shows all subjects, terms, and cumulative GPA
Grade history modal shows all revisions with editor name and timestamp

plan.description: Build the Timetable Scheduling UI: weekly timetable grid editor for admins, read-only timetable views for teachers/students, substitution assignment interface, and timetable export.
acceptance_criteria: Timetable grid displays 5-day week with configurable period slots as drag-and-drop cells
Conflict banner appears inline when a double-booking is detected on slot edit
Teacher timetable view shows personal schedule across all assigned classes
Student timetable view shows section schedule with teacher and room details
Substitution form allows selecting substitute teacher with availability check
Export to iCal downloads .ics file for calendar import

plan.description: Build the Parent Communication UI: messaging inbox/compose interface, announcement feed, notification preferences settings, and real-time message delivery indicator.
acceptance_criteria: Inbox shows threaded conversations with unread count badge
Compose interface supports recipient search by name and attachment upload
Announcement feed filters by school/grade/section scope
Notification bell shows unread count and opens notification panel
Real-time updates via WebSocket for new messages without page refresh
Notification preferences allow per-channel opt-out (email, push, in-app)

plan.description: Build the Fee Collection UI: fee structure configuration for admins, invoice listing with status, online payment checkout flow (Stripe Elements), payment history, and financial summary dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Fee structure manager supports CRUD for fee types with recurrence settings
Invoice list shows: invoice_number, amount, due_date, status with filter by status
Stripe Checkout uses Stripe Elements (no raw card data touches the server)
Payment confirmation page shows receipt number and sends email confirmation
Financial dashboard shows collected vs. outstanding vs. overdue with trend chart
Parent payment history shows all invoices and payments with PDF download per receipt

plan.description: Write unit and integration tests for all six backend modules: auth, enrollment, attendance, grades, timetable, messaging, and fees. Target ≥80% code coverage.
acceptance_criteria: All API endpoints have at least one happy-path and one error-path test
RBAC tested: each endpoint verifies unauthorized roles receive 403
Enrollment tests cover: create, update, transfer, withdrawal workflows
Attendance tests verify: immutability after 48hr, bulk upload, notification trigger
Fee tests mock Stripe API and verify webhook handling for success and failure
Test coverage report shows ≥80% for all modules
Tests run in isolation using test database with fixtures and teardown

plan.description: Produce a comprehensive QA test plan: test strategy, test cases for all six modules, regression test suite, FERPA/COPPA data handling test cases, and WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility test cases.
acceptance_criteria: Test plan covers all six modules with test objectives and scope
Minimum 10 test cases per module covering positive, negative, and edge cases
FERPA test cases verify: data access controls, audit logging, record retention
COPPA test cases verify: parental consent flows, data minimization for minors
WCAG 2.1 AA test cases cover: keyboard navigation, screen reader, color contrast, focus management
Performance test cases define SLAs: page load <2s, API response <500ms p95

plan.description: Set up CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions workflows for lint, test, build, security scan (Bandit/Trivy), and deployment to staging and production. Configure environment-based secrets management.
acceptance_criteria: CI pipeline runs on every PR: lint → test → security scan, blocks merge on failure
Docker images built and pushed to container registry on merge to main
Deploy to staging auto-triggers on merge to main
Deploy to production requires manual approval gate in GitHub Actions
Bandit scans for Python security issues; Trivy scans Docker image for CVEs
Secrets managed via GitHub Actions secrets, not hardcoded in workflow files
Pipeline run time under 10 minutes for full CI cycle

plan.description: Provision cloud infrastructure: Kubernetes cluster (or managed container service), PostgreSQL managed database, Redis cache, S3 for document storage, CDN for frontend, and monitoring stack (Prometheus + Grafana).
acceptance_criteria: All infrastructure defined as Terraform code (no manual console changes)
RDS configured with Multi-AZ, automated backups (7-day retention), encryption at rest
S3 bucket has server-side encryption, versioning enabled, and public access blocked
Kubernetes deployments have health checks, resource limits, and HPA configured
Prometheus scrapes all services; Grafana dashboards for API latency, error rate, DB connections
PagerDuty alerts fire for: p95 latency >1s, error rate >1%, DB connection exhaustion

plan.description: Execute end-to-end system integration tests across all six modules using Playwright. Cover complete user journeys: student enrollment through to fee payment, teacher marking attendance and entering grades, parent viewing report card.
acceptance_criteria: All six E2E user journeys pass on staging environment
Each journey executes in under 3 minutes
Cross-module data consistency verified (e.g., enrolled student appears in attendance)
Role isolation verified: parent cannot access admin routes
Payment E2E uses Stripe test mode with test card numbers
Test reports generated with screenshots on failure

plan.description: Execute WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit across all portal pages using automated (axe-core) and manual testing. Produce audit report with pass/fail per criterion and remediation tickets for failures.
acceptance_criteria: axe-core reports zero critical or serious violations on all audited pages
All interactive elements operable by keyboard alone
All images have meaningful alt text; decorative images have empty alt
Focus indicator visible on all focusable elements
Color contrast ratio ≥4.5:1 for normal text, ≥3:1 for large text
Screen reader (NVDA + Chrome) can complete enrollment wizard without sighted assistance
Audit report documents all 50 WCAG 2.1 AA success criteria with pass/fail/NA

plan.description: Produce user guides, training materials, and onboarding documentation for all four user roles: School Admin, Teacher, Parent, and Student. Include video scripts for key workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Admin guide covers: school setup, user management, timetable creation, financial reporting
Teacher guide covers: attendance marking, grade entry, messaging parents
Parent guide covers: viewing grades, attendance, paying fees, messaging teachers
All guides reviewed by a representative user from each role for clarity
Video scripts cover the four highest-frequency workflows with step-by-step narration cues
In-app help tooltips added to all complex form fields
FAQ addresses top 20 anticipated support questions

plan.description: Write technical documentation: API reference (auto-generated from OpenAPI spec), system architecture diagram, database ERD, deployment runbook, and developer onboarding guide.
acceptance_criteria: API reference auto-generated from openapi.yaml and hosted at /docs
Architecture diagram shows all services, data flows, and external integrations
Deployment runbook covers: new environment setup, rollback procedure, DB migration steps
Developer onboarding guide enables a new engineer to run the stack locally in under 30 minutes
CHANGELOG follows Keep a Changelog format with all MVP changes documented

flaws: Steps 13–18 (all backend modules) depend only on step 12 (auth) but NOT on step 8 (database schema). Implementing enrollment, attendance, grades, etc. before the schema is finalized means every module will likely require destructive model changes mid-implementation. This is the single most damaging structural error in the plan.

Steps 1 and 2 both have depends_on: [] but PRD (step 2) cannot realistically be produced without completed business analysis (step 1). Running them in parallel means the PRD will be based on incomplete requirements, requiring rework.

Step 5 (security/STRIDE threat model) also has depends_on: [] despite requiring module-level understanding from step 1. A threat model written before requirements are documented will miss attack surfaces.

Academic year rollover is entirely absent. At year-end, student enrollments must archive, grade history must freeze, new academic year must initialize, and section assignments must roll forward. This is a critical operational workflow for any school ERP and affects the core data model.

Step 23 specifies drag-and-drop timetable editor but timetable auto-generation (an NP-hard constraint satisfaction problem) is listed as a feature in step 16 with no algorithmic approach defined. Manual drag-and-drop for 50+ teachers, 30+ rooms, and 200+ sections is operationally unusable.

WebSocket infrastructure for real-time messaging (step 24 requirement) has no corresponding setup in step 10 (Docker Compose), step 29 (infra), or step 12 (auth). WebSockets behind Kubernetes/load balancers require sticky sessions or Redis pub/sub — neither is mentioned anywhere.

COPPA parental consent collection workflow is missing from the enrollment module (step 13). COPPA requires verifiable parental consent before data collection for children under 13. The legal step (step 3) documents the requirement but no corresponding implementation step exists.
FERPA right-of-access (parent/student right to request and receive all records) has no data export feature in scope. Step 3 documents data retention but no export-on-request workflow is planned.

Grade finalization/locking after term end is absent. 'Append-only revisions' is not the same as a term-close workflow that locks the gradebook, triggers report card generation, and computes cumulative GPA for the transcript.

Step 17 specifies Firebase Cloud Messaging but no mobile app is in scope. FCM for web browsers uses VAPID (Web Push Protocol), which is architecturally different from FCM for native mobile. This distinction is critical for the notification infrastructure design.

Age-based record access transition is unaddressed. Under FERPA, when a student turns 18, record access rights transfer from parent to student. No logic exists for this transition, which is a compliance gap for any multi-year student records.
suggestions: Fix the dependency graph: steps 13–18 must add step 8 to depends_on. Step 2 must depend on step 1. Step 5 must depend on step 1. Draw the full DAG and validate it before execution.

Add a step 0 or step 1.5 specifically for academic year data model design — define how year transitions work, what archives, what carries forward, and what initializes. This decision cascades through every module's schema.

Split timetable into two sub-steps: (a) manual slot editor with conflict detection (achievable), and (b) auto-generation with a defined algorithm (OR-Tools, backtracking, or simulated annealing). Set explicit scope for MVP vs. Phase 2 on this.

Add a Redis pub/sub or dedicated WebSocket service (e.g., Socket.IO server or FastAPI WebSocket handler behind a Redis adapter) to step 10's docker-compose and step 29's infrastructure. Without this, real-time messaging will not scale past one API instance.

Add a COPPA consent step to step 13's enrollment workflow: before creating a student record for a child under 13, the system must collect and record verifiable parental consent. Define the consent storage model and UI flow.
Add a data export API endpoint (GET /students/{id}/export returning ZIP of all FERPA-covered records) and corresponding UI to the admin portal. This is a legal obligation, not a nice-to-have.

Specify PDF generation library in step 15. WeasyPrint, ReportLab, and wkhtmltopdf have radically different capabilities, maintenance status, and Docker image size implications. Lock this choice early — report card layout will be built around it.

Add file upload security to step 13: virus/malware scanning (ClamAV or S3 event trigger to a scanning lambda), MIME-type validation server-side (not just extension check), and a maximum file size limit enforced in the API layer.

Add Stripe webhook signature verification as an explicit acceptance criterion in step 18. 'Stripe webhook handles payment_intent.succeeded' is incomplete without verifying the Stripe-Signature header — without it, the endpoint is open to spoofed payment confirmations.

Add API versioning (/api/v1/) to the OpenAPI spec in step 9. This is a multi-tenant SaaS serving multiple school districts. Without versioning, any breaking API change forces simultaneous upgrades across all tenants.

Add a Celery monitoring step (Flower or integration with Grafana via celery-exporter). Background task failures (notification delivery, PDF generation, invoice scheduling) will be invisible without this.
Define RTO and RPO in step 29. '7-day backup retention' is not a disaster recovery plan. Specify: how long to restore from backup, how to test restores, and what the acceptable data loss window is.
missing: Academic year rollover workflow (archive, transition, new-year initialization)
PCI DSS compliance documentation — even Stripe Elements SAQ A requirements are unaddressed
COPPA verifiable parental consent collection and storage workflow
FERPA data export on request (parent/student right of access)
WebSocket server/infrastructure design for real-time messaging
Timetable auto-generation algorithm specification (OR-Tools, backtracking, etc.)
Age-18 record access transfer logic (FERPA §99.5)
Grade finalization/term-close workflow
File upload malware scanning
Email bounce and delivery failure handling (dead-letter queue, retry, fallback)
Celery task monitoring and dead-letter handling
API versioning strategy
RTO/RPO definition and restore testing procedure
Stripe webhook signature verification as explicit requirement
Rate limiting implementation (mentioned in spec but no implementation step)
Data migration/import from legacy systems (existing schools have years of records)
Concurrent session management and forced logout for compromised accounts
Tenant isolation middleware (application-layer enforcement of school_id scoping, not just FK conventions)
security_risks: Stripe webhook endpoint with no explicit signature verification requirement is a critical financial fraud vector. A spoofed payment_intent.succeeded event would mark invoices as paid without actual payment.

Document upload to S3 with no server-side MIME validation or malware scanning allows upload of malicious files (polyglot PDFs, macro-enabled Office docs). Signed S3 URLs returned to users could serve malware.

Row Level Security listed as a database 'feature' but no Postgres RLS policies are specified for implementation. Without enforced RLS, a bug in application-layer filtering leaks all tenants' student PII to any authenticated user.

Multi-tenant school_id FK convention relies entirely on application-layer enforcement. A single missing WHERE clause in any query exposes cross-tenant data. No compensating control (RLS, separate schemas, or connection pooling per tenant) is specified.

XSS via user-generated content in messaging module. Teacher-to-parent messages with no output encoding or content security policy allow stored XSS. School admin moderation flag does not prevent initial rendering.

FERPA audit log tamper protection is unspecified. The audit_log table captures events, but nothing prevents a compromised admin account from deleting or modifying audit records. Append-only enforcement (Postgres trigger or separate write-only log store) is absent.

Password reset tokens stored in the database with no mention of hashing. If the token column is plaintext and the DB is breached, all active reset tokens are compromised. Tokens must be stored as bcrypt/argon2 hashes.

Rate limiting on auth endpoints (5/min per IP) is in the acceptance criteria but no implementation step exists — not in the API design step, not in the auth implementation step, not in the infra step. This is missing from the execution plan entirely.
No Content Security Policy (CSP) headers specified anywhere. An ERP handling student PII with no CSP is vulnerable to data exfiltration via injected scripts.
SSRF risk: document upload pre-signed URL generation or any URL-fetching feature could be abused to probe internal AWS metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254). No SSRF mitigation is mentioned.
final_review.summary: This is a structurally ambitious and surprisingly detailed plan for a 6-module School ERP with genuine attention to FERPA/COPPA compliance, RBAC, multi-tenancy, and accessibility — qualities that many ERP plans skip entirely. However, it has a critical dependency graph bug: all six backend implementation steps (13–18) depend only on auth (step 12) and not on the database schema (step 8), meaning the schema can be changed after modules are half-built, guaranteeing destructive rework. Beyond the graph, there are three production-blocking gaps: (1) no academic year rollover workflow, which is a fundamental operational requirement for any school system; (2) no COPPA consent collection implementation despite the legal step identifying it; and (3) no WebSocket infrastructure despite requiring real-time messaging. The fee collection module has a PCI DSS blind spot — Stripe Elements reduces scope but does not eliminate it, and no SAQ A compliance work is planned. The timetable auto-generation feature is aspirationally listed but algorithmically unspecified, making it effectively undeliverable as described. For an MVP that scoped down to enrollment, attendance, and grades (as the PRD step suggests), this plan would score in the mid-70s after fixing the dependency graph. As a full 6-module production plan ready for school districts handling regulated student PII, it needs the identified gaps closed before execution.

flaws: Steps 13–18 (all backend modules) depend only on step 12 (auth) but NOT on step 8 (database schema). Implementing enrollment, attendance, grades, etc. before the schema is finalized means every module will likely require destructive model changes mid-implementation. This is the single most damaging structural error in the plan.

Steps 1 and 2 both have depends_on: [] but PRD (step 2) cannot realistically be produced without completed business analysis (step 1). Running them in parallel means the PRD will be based on incomplete requirements, requiring rework.

Step 5 (security/STRIDE threat model) also has depends_on: [] despite requiring module-level understanding from step 1. A threat model written before requirements are documented will miss attack surfaces.

Academic year rollover is entirely absent. At year-end, student enrollments must archive, grade history must freeze, new academic year must initialize, and section assignments must roll forward. This is a critical operational workflow for any school ERP and affects the core data model.

Step 23 specifies drag-and-drop timetable editor but timetable auto-generation (an NP-hard constraint satisfaction problem) is listed as a feature in step 16 with no algorithmic approach defined. Manual drag-and-drop for 50+ teachers, 30+ rooms, and 200+ sections is operationally unusable.

WebSocket infrastructure for real-time messaging (step 24 requirement) has no corresponding setup in step 10 (Docker Compose), step 29 (infra), or step 12 (auth). WebSockets behind Kubernetes/load balancers require sticky sessions or Redis pub/sub — neither is mentioned anywhere.

COPPA parental consent collection workflow is missing from the enrollment module (step 13). COPPA requires verifiable parental consent before data collection for children under 13. The legal step (step 3) documents the requirement but no corresponding implementation step exists.
FERPA right-of-access (parent/student right to request and receive all records) has no data export feature in scope. Step 3 documents data retention but no export-on-request workflow is planned.

Grade finalization/locking after term end is absent. 'Append-only revisions' is not the same as a term-close workflow that locks the gradebook, triggers report card generation, and computes cumulative GPA for the transcript.

Step 17 specifies Firebase Cloud Messaging but no mobile app is in scope. FCM for web browsers uses VAPID (Web Push Protocol), which is architecturally different from FCM for native mobile. This distinction is critical for the notification infrastructure design.

Age-based record access transition is unaddressed. Under FERPA, when a student turns 18, record access rights transfer from parent to student. No logic exists for this transition, which is a compliance gap for any multi-year student records.
suggestions: Fix the dependency graph: steps 13–18 must add step 8 to depends_on. Step 2 must depend on step 1. Step 5 must depend on step 1. Draw the full DAG and validate it before execution.

Add a step 0 or step 1.5 specifically for academic year data model design — define how year transitions work, what archives, what carries forward, and what initializes. This decision cascades through every module's schema.

Split timetable into two sub-steps: (a) manual slot editor with conflict detection (achievable), and (b) auto-generation with a defined algorithm (OR-Tools, backtracking, or simulated annealing). Set explicit scope for MVP vs. Phase 2 on this.

Add a Redis pub/sub or dedicated WebSocket service (e.g., Socket.IO server or FastAPI WebSocket handler behind a Redis adapter) to step 10's docker-compose and step 29's infrastructure. Without this, real-time messaging will not scale past one API instance.

Add a COPPA consent step to step 13's enrollment workflow: before creating a student record for a child under 13, the system must collect and record verifiable parental consent. Define the consent storage model and UI flow.
Add a data export API endpoint (GET /students/{id}/export returning ZIP of all FERPA-covered records) and corresponding UI to the admin portal. This is a legal obligation, not a nice-to-have.

Specify PDF generation library in step 15. WeasyPrint, ReportLab, and wkhtmltopdf have radically different capabilities, maintenance status, and Docker image size implications. Lock this choice early — report card layout will be built around it.

Add file upload security to step 13: virus/malware scanning (ClamAV or S3 event trigger to a scanning lambda), MIME-type validation server-side (not just extension check), and a maximum file size limit enforced in the API layer.

Add Stripe webhook signature verification as an explicit acceptance criterion in step 18. 'Stripe webhook handles payment_intent.succeeded' is incomplete without verifying the Stripe-Signature header — without it, the endpoint is open to spoofed payment confirmations.

Add API versioning (/api/v1/) to the OpenAPI spec in step 9. This is a multi-tenant SaaS serving multiple school districts. Without versioning, any breaking API change forces simultaneous upgrades across all tenants.

Add a Celery monitoring step (Flower or integration with Grafana via celery-exporter). Background task failures (notification delivery, PDF generation, invoice scheduling) will be invisible without this.
Define RTO and RPO in step 29. '7-day backup retention' is not a disaster recovery plan. Specify: how long to restore from backup, how to test restores, and what the acceptable data loss window is.
missing: Academic year rollover workflow (archive, transition, new-year initialization)
PCI DSS compliance documentation — even Stripe Elements SAQ A requirements are unaddressed
COPPA verifiable parental consent collection and storage workflow
FERPA data export on request (parent/student right of access)
WebSocket server/infrastructure design for real-time messaging
Timetable auto-generation algorithm specification (OR-Tools, backtracking, etc.)
Age-18 record access transfer logic (FERPA §99.5)
Grade finalization/term-close workflow
File upload malware scanning
Email bounce and delivery failure handling (dead-letter queue, retry, fallback)
Celery task monitoring and dead-letter handling
API versioning strategy
RTO/RPO definition and restore testing procedure
Stripe webhook signature verification as explicit requirement
Rate limiting implementation (mentioned in spec but no implementation step)
Data migration/import from legacy systems (existing schools have years of records)
Concurrent session management and forced logout for compromised accounts
Tenant isolation middleware (application-layer enforcement of school_id scoping, not just FK conventions)
security_risks: Stripe webhook endpoint with no explicit signature verification requirement is a critical financial fraud vector. A spoofed payment_intent.succeeded event would mark invoices as paid without actual payment.

Document upload to S3 with no server-side MIME validation or malware scanning allows upload of malicious files (polyglot PDFs, macro-enabled Office docs). Signed S3 URLs returned to users could serve malware.

Row Level Security listed as a database 'feature' but no Postgres RLS policies are specified for implementation. Without enforced RLS, a bug in application-layer filtering leaks all tenants' student PII to any authenticated user.

Multi-tenant school_id FK convention relies entirely on application-layer enforcement. A single missing WHERE clause in any query exposes cross-tenant data. No compensating control (RLS, separate schemas, or connection pooling per tenant) is specified.

XSS via user-generated content in messaging module. Teacher-to-parent messages with no output encoding or content security policy allow stored XSS. School admin moderation flag does not prevent initial rendering.

FERPA audit log tamper protection is unspecified. The audit_log table captures events, but nothing prevents a compromised admin account from deleting or modifying audit records. Append-only enforcement (Postgres trigger or separate write-only log store) is absent.

Password reset tokens stored in the database with no mention of hashing. If the token column is plaintext and the DB is breached, all active reset tokens are compromised. Tokens must be stored as bcrypt/argon2 hashes.

Rate limiting on auth endpoints (5/min per IP) is in the acceptance criteria but no implementation step exists — not in the API design step, not in the auth implementation step, not in the infra step. This is missing from the execution plan entirely.
No Content Security Policy (CSP) headers specified anywhere. An ERP handling student PII with no CSP is vulnerable to data exfiltration via injected scripts.
SSRF risk: document upload pre-signed URL generation or any URL-fetching feature could be abused to probe internal AWS metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254). No SSRF mitigation is mentioned.
history.summary: This is a structurally ambitious and surprisingly detailed plan for a 6-module School ERP with genuine attention to FERPA/COPPA compliance, RBAC, multi-tenancy, and accessibility — qualities that many ERP plans skip entirely. However, it has a critical dependency graph bug: all six backend implementation steps (13–18) depend only on auth (step 12) and not on the database schema (step 8), meaning the schema can be changed after modules are half-built, guaranteeing destructive rework. Beyond the graph, there are three production-blocking gaps: (1) no academic year rollover workflow, which is a fundamental operational requirement for any school system; (2) no COPPA consent collection implementation despite the legal step identifying it; and (3) no WebSocket infrastructure despite requiring real-time messaging. The fee collection module has a PCI DSS blind spot — Stripe Elements reduces scope but does not eliminate it, and no SAQ A compliance work is planned. The timetable auto-generation feature is aspirationally listed but algorithmically unspecified, making it effectively undeliverable as described. For an MVP that scoped down to enrollment, attendance, and grades (as the PRD step suggests), this plan would score in the mid-70s after fixing the dependency graph. As a full 6-module production plan ready for school districts handling regulated student PII, it needs the identified gaps closed before execution.
